title: Summer Slamajama: A Summer Gallery Opening
location: Anne Irwin Fine Art, 690 Miami Cir NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Open-air summer art opening with a DJ and refreshments. A relaxed, walk-around kind of date — easy conversation starter and a chance to pick out something for the apartment together.

title: Screenings on The Green — Outdoor Movie Night
location: The Green at High Street Atlanta, 62 N Main St, Alpharetta, GA
description: Bring a blanket and a date to a complimentary outdoor movie screening on the lawn with free popcorn. Free, no-pressure Thursday-night date that pairs with dinner and drinks on the High Street boardwalk.

title: Na'im Lynn at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: A polished headliner in a small-room club setting — exactly the right vibe for a first date or a 'let's get out of the house' weeknight. Cheap enough to grab dinner before or drinks after.

title: ATLANTA'S AFRO CARIBBEAN FEST
location: Embr Lounge, 1136 Crescent Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
price: Free entry before 5 PM w/ RSVP (21+)
description: Indoor + outdoor dancefloor with DJs spinning 90s/00s reggae, Afrobeats, and R&B. Late afternoon energy, daylight dancing, full kitchen + drink specials — couples who want to move together without the late-night club scene.

title: Music in the Garden — Atlanta Botanical Garden
location: Atlanta Botanical Garden, 1345 Piedmont Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Live outdoor music in the garden as the sun goes down. Walk the trails, listen to the band, and grab a drink at the outdoor bar — peak Atlanta summer date energy, the kind that feels like a movie scene.

title: Prima Materia — Group Exhibition Opening
location: Empire Arts Gallery, Atlanta, GA
description: The origin point of transformation — artists reveal the sacred beginning of the creative act where pigment, gesture, and intention emerge from the unknown. A quiet, intentional gallery walk that's a great excuse to hold hands and talk about what you see.

title: NE-YO & AKON: Nights Like This Tour 2026
location: Lakewood Amphitheatre, 2002 Lakewood Way SW, Atlanta, GA
description: Two R&B heavyweights on one stage — pure 2000s nostalgia singalong. Tailgate-style date, bring a blanket and a bottle of wine, stay for the slow jams.

title: Life Space Vegan Chef Cook-Off
location: 294 Hamilton E Holmes Dr NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Taste, vote, and celebrate at this plant-based chef showdown. Perfect for a foodie couple — walk the booths together, compare notes, and pick a winner. A built-in conversation loop and zero date-night pressure.

title: PeachFest 2026
location: Peachtree Center, 303 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Annual celebration of Atlanta's iconic fruit with food vendors, live music, and a market. Sticky, sweet, summer-vibes date in the heart of downtown.

title: Poetic Jazz: Prince Tribute Night
location: The Vick at Healey, 57 Forsyth St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: An intimate jazz-poetry spin on Prince's catalog. Candles, cocktails, and a small downtown crowd — the kind of low-key, soulful night that turns a Tuesday into a memory.

title: Cocktails in the Garden — Atlanta Botanical Garden
location: Atlanta Botanical Garden, 1345 Piedmont Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Cocktails, live DJ, and the gardens lit up at golden hour. A timeless Atlanta date formula — wander the trails, grab a drink, sit by the fountain. Easy to dress up or down.

title: Karlous Miller at Helium Comedy Club (Couple's Package)
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
price: Couple's Package: 2 premium seats + $90 food & beverage credit
description: 85 South / Wild 'N Out star Karlous Miller in an intimate showroom — the venue literally sells a Couple's Package with reserved seating and a $90 F&B credit. Premium, no-stress date night with a guaranteed laugh.

title: Savanna Nights at Zoo Atlanta — Boy Bands & Girl Groups
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Cocktails, animal encounters, and a live Boy Bands & Girl Groups tribute set, all in the zoo after hours. Built-in 'first date' energy — fun, walkable, and full of moments to point and laugh at together.

title: Steel Pulse
location: The Eastern, 800 Old Flat Shoals Rd SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Legendary British roots-reggae band on a U.S. tour — deep grooves, conscious lyrics, and a converted-warehouse room that's perfect for slow-dancing close in the back.

title: Phillippia and Yoshi — R&B Dinner & a Show
location: Perfect Note Atlanta, 1520 Federal Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: An intimate R&B dinner-and-a-show experience. Order off the menu, candles on the tables, two voices that can actually sing — the kind of date that feels like an anniversary without trying.

title: Lionel Richie & Earth, Wind & Fire — Sing a Song All Night Long
location: State Farm Arena, 1 State Farm Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Two Hall of Fame acts, one night of 'All Night Long' / 'September' energy. Bring a partner who likes to dance in the aisle — the whole arena becomes a wedding reception by the encore.

title: Rooftop Salsa & Bachata Social
location: Atlanta (rooftop venue, TBA)
description: A weekend rooftop salsa + bachata social — beginner-friendly floor, drinks with a view, and instructors on hand so you can take a lesson together before showing off (or laughing at each other).

title: Amy Sherald: American Sublime — High Museum of Art
location: High Museum of Art, 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: The final stop on the national tour of Georgia native Amy Sherald's most comprehensive exhibition to date. A meaningful, conversation-rich date — slow walk, big paintings, lunch in Midtown after.

title: James "Murr" Murray at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: Impractical Jokers' Murr headlines a late-July weekend — goofy, storytelling-style stand-up that lands as fun group-date energy even for a duo. Pair with dinner at the boardwalk.

title: Buju Banton & Stephen Marley: Roots and Rhymes Tour
location: Cellairis Amphitheatre at Lakewood, 2002 Lakewood Way SW, Atlanta, GA
description: A reggae double-header on the lawn — Bob Marley's son and a living legend together. Bring a blanket, bring a cooler, stay for the singalongs under the stars.

title: INNER VIEWS Art Exhibition — Opening
location: One Contemporary Gallery, 395 Edgewood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: A collective musing on home, family, and community by 14 contemporary artists across multiple mediums. Quiet, thoughtful Saturday-afternoon gallery date in Edgewood — pair with lunch on the beltline.

title: Savanna Nights at Zoo Atlanta — Decades Night
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: The final Savanna Nights of the summer — cocktails, animal encounters, and a Decades-night live set. Last-call romantic before the season closes; a memorable 'I can't believe we did that' kind of date.

title: Dinner and A Date: A Night Of Soul Live Music
location: Apache XLR, Atlanta, GA
description: A dedicated date-night soul show — the entire event is built around couples, dining, and live R&B. Easy, on-theme, and Sunday-evening slow-dance material.
